child.name: resourceType
child.description: This is a ImplementationGuide resource

child.name: id
child.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

child.name: meta
child.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not alwa...
child.longDesc: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: versionId
child.description: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is create...
child.longDesc: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is created, updated, or deleted.

child.name: lastUpdated
child.description: When the resource last changed - e.g. when the version changed.

child.name: source
child.description: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can...
child.longDesc: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can be used to track or differentiate the source of information in the resource. The source may identify another FHIR server, document, message, database, etc.

child.name: profile
child.description: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a ref...
child.longDesc: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a reference to [[[StructureDefinition.url]]].

child.name: security
child.description: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructur...
child.longDesc: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructure.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: tag
child.description: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applic...
child.longDesc: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applications are not required to consider the tags when interpreting the meaning of a resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: implicitRules
child.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing...
child.longDesc: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

child.name: language
child.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

child.name: text
child.description: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a...
child.longDesc: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: status
child.description: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a h...
child.longDesc: The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a human authored it and it may contain additional data.

child.name: div
child.description: The actual narrative content, a stripped down version of XHTML.

child.name: url
child.description: An absolute URI that is used to identify this implementation guide when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an...
child.longDesc: An absolute URI that is used to identify this implementation guide when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which an authoritative instance of this implementation guide is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the implementation guide is stored on different servers.

child.name: identifier
child.description: A formal identifier that is used to identify this implementation guide when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in...
child.longDesc: A formal identifier that is used to identify this implementation guide when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: version
child.description: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the implementation guide when it is referenced in a specification, model...
child.longDesc: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the implementation guide when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the implementation guide author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

child.name: versionAlgorithmString
child.description: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which is more current.

child.name: versionAlgorithmCoding
child.description: Indicates the mechanism used to compare versions to determine which is more current.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: name
child.description: A natural language name identifying the implementation guide. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by mach...
child.longDesc: A natural language name identifying the implementation guide. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

child.name: title
child.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the implementation guide.

child.name: status
child.description: The status of this implementation guide. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

child.name: experimental
child.description: A Boolean value to indicate that this implementation guide is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) ...
child.longDesc: A Boolean value to indicate that this implementation guide is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

child.name: date
child.description: The date (and optionally time) when the implementation guide was last significantly changed. The date must change when the busi...
child.longDesc: The date (and optionally time) when the implementation guide was last significantly changed. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the implementation guide changes.

child.name: publisher
child.description: The name of the organization or individual responsible for the release and ongoing maintenance of the implementation guide.

child.name: contact
child.description: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: The name of an individual to contact.

child.name: telecom
child.description: The contact details for the individual (if a name was provided) or the organization.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: Telecommunications form for contact point - what communications system is required to make use of the contact.

child.name: value
child.description: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or emai...
child.longDesc: The actual contact point details, in a form that is meaningful to the designated communication system (i.e. phone number or email address).

child.name: use
child.description: Identifies the purpose for the contact point.

child.name: rank
child.description: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than thos...
child.longDesc: Specifies a preferred order in which to use a set of contacts. ContactPoints with lower rank values are more preferred than those with higher rank values.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period when the contact point was/is in use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: description
child.description: A free text natural language description of the implementation guide from a consumer's perspective.

child.name: useContext
child.description: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general cate...
child.longDesc: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate implementation guide instances.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: A code that identifies the type of context being specified by this usage context.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: valueCodeableConcept
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: valueQuantity
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: valueRange
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: low
child.description: The low limit. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: high
child.description: The high limit. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: value
child.description: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.

child.name: comparator
child.description: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to me...
child.longDesc: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.

child.name: unit
child.description: A human-readable form of the unit.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.

child.name: code
child.description: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.

child.name: valueReference
child.description: A value that defines the context specified in this context of use. The interpretation of the value is defined by the code.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: jurisdiction
child.description: A legal or geographic region in which the implementation guide is intended to be used.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: purpose
child.description: Explanation of why this implementation guide is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

child.name: copyright
child.description: A copyright statement relating to the implementation guide and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restric...
child.longDesc: A copyright statement relating to the implementation guide and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the implementation guide.

child.name: copyrightLabel
child.description: A short string (<50 characters), suitable for inclusion in a page footer that identifies the copyright holder, effective period,...
child.longDesc: A short string (<50 characters), suitable for inclusion in a page footer that identifies the copyright holder, effective period, and optionally whether rights are resctricted. (e.g. 'All rights reserved', 'Some rights reserved').

child.name: packageId
child.description: The NPM package name for this Implementation Guide, used in the NPM package distribution, which is the primary mechanism by whic...
child.longDesc: The NPM package name for this Implementation Guide, used in the NPM package distribution, which is the primary mechanism by which FHIR based tooling manages IG dependencies. This value must be globally unique, and should be assigned with care.

child.name: license
child.description: The license that applies to this Implementation Guide, using an SPDX license code, or 'not-open-source'.

child.name: fhirVersion
child.description: The version(s) of the FHIR specification that this ImplementationGuide targets - e.g. describes how to use. The value of this el...
child.longDesc: The version(s) of the FHIR specification that this ImplementationGuide targets - e.g. describes how to use. The value of this element is the formal version of the specification, without the revision number, e.g. [publication].[major].[minor], which is 4.6.0. for this version.

child.name: dependsOn
child.description: Another implementation guide that this implementation depends on. Typically, an implementation guide uses value sets, profiles e...
child.longDesc: Another implementation guide that this implementation depends on. Typically, an implementation guide uses value sets, profiles etc.defined in other implementation guides.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: uri
child.description: A canonical reference to the Implementation guide for the dependency.

child.name: packageId
child.description: The NPM package name for the Implementation Guide that this IG depends on.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the IG that is depended on, when the correct version is required to understand the IG correctly.

child.name: reason
child.description: A description explaining the nature of the dependency on the listed IG.

child.name: global
child.description: A set of profiles that all resources covered by this implementation guide must conform to.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: type
child.description: The type of resource that all instances must conform to.

child.name: profile
child.description: A reference to the profile that all instances must conform to.

child.name: definition
child.description: The information needed by an IG publisher tool to publish the whole implementation guide.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: grouping
child.description: A logical group of resources. Logical groups can be used when building pages.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: The human-readable title to display for the package of resources when rendering the implementation guide.

child.name: description
child.description: Human readable text describing the package.

child.name: resource
child.description: A resource that is part of the implementation guide. Conformance resources (value set, structure definition, capability statemen...
child.longDesc: A resource that is part of the implementation guide. Conformance resources (value set, structure definition, capability statements etc.) are obvious candidates for inclusion, but any kind of resource can be included as an example resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: Where this resource is found.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: fhirVersion
child.description: Indicates the FHIR Version(s) this artifact is intended to apply to. If no versions are specified, the resource is assumed to ap...
child.longDesc: Indicates the FHIR Version(s) this artifact is intended to apply to. If no versions are specified, the resource is assumed to apply to all the versions stated in ImplementationGuide.fhirVersion.

child.name: name
child.description: A human assigned name for the resource. All resources SHOULD have a name, but the name may be extracted from the resource (e.g. ...
child.longDesc: A human assigned name for the resource. All resources SHOULD have a name, but the name may be extracted from the resource (e.g. ValueSet.name).

child.name: description
child.description: A description of the reason that a resource has been included in the implementation guide.

child.name: isExample
child.description: If true, indicates the resource is an example instance.

child.name: profile
child.description: If present, indicates profile(s) the instance is valid against.

child.name: groupingId
child.description: Reference to the id of the grouping this resource appears in.

child.name: page
child.description: A page / section in the implementation guide. The root page is the implementation guide home page.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: sourceUrl
child.description: Indicates the URL or the actual content to provide for the page.

child.name: sourceString
child.description: Indicates the URL or the actual content to provide for the page.

child.name: sourceMarkdown
child.description: Indicates the URL or the actual content to provide for the page.

child.name: name
child.description: The url by which the page should be known when published.

child.name: title
child.description: A short title used to represent this page in navigational structures such as table of contents, bread crumbs, etc.

child.name: generation
child.description: A code that indicates how the page is generated.

child.name: page
child.description: Nested Pages/Sections under this page.

child.name: parameter
child.description: A set of parameters that defines how the implementation guide is built. The parameters are defined by the relevant tools that bu...
child.longDesc: A set of parameters that defines how the implementation guide is built. The parameters are defined by the relevant tools that build the implementation guides.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: A tool-specific code that defines the parameter.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: value
child.description: Value for named type.

child.name: template
child.description: A template for building resources.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: code
child.description: Type of template specified.

child.name: source
child.description: The source location for the template.

child.name: scope
child.description: The scope in which the template applies.

child.name: manifest
child.description: Information about an assembled implementation guide, created by the publication tooling.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: rendering
child.description: A pointer to official web page, PDF or other rendering of the implementation guide.

child.name: resource
child.description: A resource that is part of the implementation guide. Conformance resources (value set, structure definition, capability statemen...
child.longDesc: A resource that is part of the implementation guide. Conformance resources (value set, structure definition, capability statements etc.) are obvious candidates for inclusion, but any kind of resource can be included as an example resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: Where this resource is found.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: isExample
child.description: If true, indicates the resource is an example instance.

child.name: profile
child.description: If present, indicates profile(s) the instance is valid against.

child.name: relativePath
child.description: The relative path for primary page for this resource within the IG.

child.name: page
child.description: Information about a page within the IG.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: name
child.description: Relative path to the page.

child.name: title
child.description: Label for the page intended for human display.

child.name: anchor
child.description: The name of an anchor available on the page.

child.name: image
child.description: Indicates a relative path to an image that exists within the IG.

child.name: other
child.description: Indicates the relative path of an additional non-page, non-image file that is part of the IG - e.g. zip, jar and similar files t...
child.longDesc: Indicates the relative path of an additional non-page, non-image file that is part of the IG - e.g. zip, jar and similar files that could be the target of a hyperlink in a derived IG.
